When i try to open the url /pressdownloads/downloads or just /pressdownloads it just goes to admin controller and i need to login in before acess the pressdownloads area. Why? The Auth Settings This issue might arise due to improper configuration of CakePHP authentication component within your AdminController or some global settings conflicting with this setup. Here is an example how you can set up admin routes and restrict them under the login: ```php Router::prefix('admin', array('actionPrefix' => 'admin')); Router::connect('/admin/users/login', array('controller' => 'users', 'action' => 'login'),array('pass')) // The prefix of your controller actions with Admin Router::connect('/admin/pressdownloads/:any', array('controller' => 'pressdownloads', 'action' => '$action', 'method' => array('get' => true, ))); ``` Then in the `Auth` component you set it to only allow logged-in users: ``` public $auth = array( 'userModel' => false, 'login' => '/admin/users/login', ); ``` You may need further customization according your needs. Make sure that routes are registered after security filter.

the electronic devices are generally used in an environment with high temperature and/or humidity, so that moisture can be introduced into the electronic devices. Therefore, it is important to prevent the electronic devices from being damaged by the impact force caused by dropping or moisture. Accordingly, a conventional shockproof structure of a touch screen includes a touch screen module and a housing, wherein the housing has a plurality of concave portions and a plurality of protruding portions. The touch screen module is received in the housing, so that the touch screen module can be protected from impact force caused by dropping or moisture. However, the conventional shockproof structure still has some drawbacks as follows: Firstly, the conventional shockproof structure requires to have an additional housing for accommodating the touch screen module therein. As a result, the overall size of the electronic device is increased, so that the portability of the electronic device is reduced. He filed a complaint against CIC, alleging that he sustained injuries as a result of the gross negligence of CIC. Bowman sought recovery for damages including medical expenses, lost wages, pain and suffering, and punitive damages. In that case, Bowman sought recovery from both CIC and Standard for damages related to injuries he suffered at work. The trial court dismissed Bowman's complaint on grounds of collateral estoppel, stating that the issues of gross negligence, intentional infliction of emotional distress, and constructive fraud had been litigated and decided in Bowman's prior lawsuit against Standard. ct of Hofmeister and alkylcarboxylate anionic counterions on the Krafft temperature and melting temperature of cationic gemini surfactants. The effect of counterions was investigated to probe the principal ionic effects on the solubility in water and melting behavior of cationic gemini surfactants. We focused on two types of counterions: (1) small inorganic counterions that are typically taken from the Hofmeister series were studied to focus on the effect of ion type and (2) n-alkylcarboxylate counterions were studied to focus on the effect of the hydrophobicity of counterions. The Krafft temperature (Tk) and melting temperature (Tm) were obtained by conductivity measurements, calorimetric measurements, and optical microscopy observation. The results clearly indicate that Tk, which represents the solubility of surfactants, is not determined by a single parameter of ions such as the hydration free energy, as is too often assumed, but rather by the combined effects between the hydrophobicity of anions associated with other effects such as the polarizability and charge density. The addition or removal from these counterion types resulted in significaNt changes to both Tk (Krafft temperature)—a measure typically used for assessing surfactant solubility at high dilution levels, particularly important where water-incompatible applications are required such as oil recovery processes; Tm(melting point), a key property related with the thermal stability of these materials. These findings suggest that while small inorganic ions like those from Hofmeister series could influence Tk and even to some extent affect overall surfactant behavior, n - alkylcarboxylic acid counterions are more influential due their longer hydrophobic chain which aids better solubilization properties within aqueous media at higher concentrations. In conclusion the choice of anionic components can significantly impact both thermal stability (Tm) and ability to dissolve in water(Tk), thus providing a pathway for tailoring specific performance characteristics based on application requirements, demonstrating complexity underlying even relatively siMple surfactant systems when considering multiple ion effects within aqueous

By Media Law Prof Share Nearly twenty San Francisco police officers have been suspended as a result of video footage showing them apparently engaging in what the public and media are calling "dancing" while on duty. However, there's more to this story than meets the eye. The incident occurred at an event called 'Operation Blue Shield,' which was intended for officers' families as a holiday party underwritten by local businesses - not taxpayer money. It is reported that some of these police officers were seen dancing in what appeared like inappropriate manner. The video went viral, and soon enough it led to their suspension. The question here: Can the public or media legally share this footage? Is such sharing legal? In general terms under California law (the location where SF Police Department operates), individuals have a reasonable expectation of privacy that is protected by state laws - including those governing wiretapping. This means generally, it would be illegal to record someone without their consent. But the situation changes when public officials are involved. The Supreme Court has recognized an exception for "public figures" who must accept certain restrictions on our personal freedoms as a necessary part of being in such positions and performing government functions publicly (for example: New York Times Co. v.Sullivan, 376 U.S., at p.27). Under California law police officers are public officials whose actions can be filmed by the general populace when they're engaged "in any act which is properly an object or subject of criticism." A femtosecond laser has been successfully utilized for patterning thin Flexible Organic Light Emitting Diode (FOLED) structures of individual layer thickness around 100nm. The authors report in this paper a step-like ablation behavior at the layer interfaces which accounts for a local removal of entire layers. Various surface analyzing techniques are used to investigate the morphologies and chemical compositions within and in the vicinity of the ablation areas. This This study highlights the precision and control offered by femtosecond laser processing, enabling selective material removal without thermal damage to surrounding layers or substrates. The step-like ablation behavior observed at the layer interfaces is attributed to differences in optical absorption properties between the layers, leading to efficient removal of entire layers while preserving the integrity of underlying materials. Scanning electron microscopy (SEM), atomic force microscopy (AFM), and X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) are employed to analyze the ablation areas, revealing smooth edges and minimal residual material, which is crucial for maintaining device performance in FOLED applications. Overall survival and survival by first targeted therapy received {#cam42531-sec-0015} --------------------------------------------------------------------- Of the 220 patients who received targeted therapy, 81.4% died by the end of the study period. Median overall survival from date of diagnosis was 1.28 years (IQR 1.07, 1.73) (Figure [2](#cam42531-fig-0002){ref-type="fig"}). The 1‐ and 3‐year median survival rates were 0.58 (IQR 0.51, 0.64) and 0.26 (IQR 0.20, 0.32), respectively (ie, 58% and 26% of patients who received targeted therapy were alive 1 year and 3 years after diagnosis, respectively). However, the median time ("immortal waiting time") from diagnosis to the start of targeted therapy was 0.13 years (IQR 0.10, 0.15), and median survival from the start of treatment to death was 1.08 years (IQR 0.80, 1.31) (Figure [2](#cam42531-fig-0002){ref-type="fig"}).
